sourceMovie.title: Roofman
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Roofman works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Old Man & the Gun
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Roofman clicked for you, The Old Man & the Gun (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Roofman?
faq.answer: Try The Old Man & the Gun, The Sugarland Express, Panic Room—each shares DNA with Roofman while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Roofman?
faq.answer: Roofman (2025) is best described as Crime, Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Roofman worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
